Title: Spearmint R2R3-MYB transcription factor MsMYB negatively regulates monoterpene production and suppresses the expression of geranyl diphosphate synthase large subunit

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: monoterpene biosynthesis; plastidial MEP-derived terpene pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses IPP and DMAPP to generate geranyl diphosphate, the C10 precursor for monoterpene biosynthesis; in spearmint functions as the catalytic LSU of heteromeric GPPS.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step for monoterpene flux in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed in peltate glandular trichomes (PGTs); light responsive
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in PGTs; transcript reduced in dark-grown plants
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Peltate glandular trichomes of aerial tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher MsGPPS.LSU expression is associated with increased monoterpene production; repression of MsGPPS.LSU is associated with reduced monoterpene production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsGPPS.LSU was the only tested terpene-related transcript significantly altered by manipulation of MsMYB in spearmint. 2. MsGPPS.LSU transcript increased in MsMYB-RNAi lines and decreased in MsMYB-overexpressing lines. 3. Its promoter contains two MYB-binding sites and is directly bound and repressed by MsMYB. 4. MsGPPS.LSU localizes to chloroplasts and is additionally regulated by light.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in transgenic lines; promoter cloning; yeast one-hybrid assay; transient promoter repression assay; subcellular localization; light/dark expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: short-chain prenyltransferase; heteromeric GPPS catalytic large subunit
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The Discussion states spearmint heteromeric GPPS consists of small and large subunits; interaction with SSU helps shape the active site toward C10-GPP production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A useful engineering target to increase monoterpene essential oil yield, especially together with derepression of MsMYB or coordinated manipulation of GPPS subunits.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native MsGPPS.LSU promoter for reporter assays; 35S promoter used for upstream MsMYB manipulation

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: GPP supply linked to mono- and sesquiterpene production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted GPPS large-subunit enzyme supplying GPP precursor for terpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not directly established, but expression correlates with reduced terpene accumulation under MsMYB ectopic expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in MsMYB ectopic-expression basil lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced ObGPPS.LSU expression is associated with decreased monoterpene and sesquiterpene accumulation in basil expressing MsMYB
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In sweet basil ectopically expressing MsMYB, ObGPPS.LSU transcript was significantly reduced. 2. The reduction coincided with strong decreases in total terpenes, including monoterpenes and sesquiterpenes. 3. ObFPPS transcript and phenylpropanoid/flavonoid outputs were unchanged, supporting a terpene-focused effect via GPPS-related precursor supply.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous overexpression of MsMYB in basil; qRT-PCR; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core pathway gene because its transcript was directly examined in the Results as a likely mediator of the terpene phenotype in applied basil plants, although direct binding by MsMYB was not tested in basil.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering basil terpene oil output by boosting GPPS expression or avoiding repression by negative regulators.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Smaller leaf size occurred in MsMYB ectopic-expression basil plants, though not assigned specifically to ObGPPS.LSU manipulation.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Mentha spicata; Ocimum basilicum; Nicotiana sylvestris
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: monoterpenes in spearmint; total terpenes in basil; cembranoid diterpenes in Nicotiana sylvestris
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: terpene biosynthesis via transcriptional repression of precursor-supplying pathway gene
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in peltate glandular trichomes (PGTs) relative to leaves, leaves stripped of PGTs, and roots
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In spearmint, enriched in PGTs; in transgenic systems ectopically expressed under 35S promoter
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Peltate glandular trichomes / glandular trichomes of aerial tissues
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses MsGPPS.LSU expression; reduces ObGPPS.LSU transcript in basil upon ectopic expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsMYB is a PGT-enriched R2R3-MYB transcription factor from spearmint. 2. RNAi knockdown increased total monoterpenes 2.3- to 4.5-fold, whereas overexpression reduced monoterpenes to 0.5- to 0.7-fold of WT. 3. MsMYB directly binds two MYB-binding sites in the MsGPPS.LSU promoter and represses promoter activity. 4. Ectopic expression in sweet basil and Nicotiana sylvestris reduced terpene-derived metabolites, while phenylpropanoid-derived eugenol and total flavonoids were unchanged in basil. 5. MsMYB is likely not regulated by miR858 under the tested conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light indirectly relevant through downstream target MsGPPS.LSU; heterologous expression in basil and tobacco/N. sylvestris
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi and overexpression in spearmint; GC-MS metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; yeast one-hybrid; transient transactivation/repression assay; subcellular localization; heterologous overexpression in basil and N. sylvestris
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (overexpression and RNAi); native MsMYB promoter used in GUS assays
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: A negative regulator that acts as a key transcriptional brake on terpene production, especially monoterpene accumulation in spearmint
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses monoterpene production in spearmint; ectopic expression suppresses terpene-derived metabolite accumulation in basil and N. sylvestris
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Promoter contains hormone-responsive cis-elements for ABA, auxin, and MeJA; probable trichome-specific transcriptional regulation; miR858 targeting not supported experimentally
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MsMYB falls in MYB subgroup 7 but unlike canonical subgroup-7 flavonoid regulators, it did not measurably affect phenylpropanoid/flavonoid outputs in this study. Overexpression in basil and tobacco/N. sylvestris caused smaller leaves, suggesting possible effects on terpene-derived hormones or additional targets.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Silencing or editing MsMYB is a promising strategy to elevate terpene essential oil yield in mint; caution is needed for heterologous deployment because overexpression caused growth-related leaf-size defects in basil and tobacco.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression in sweet basil and Nicotiana sylvestris caused smaller leaf size; no obvious morphological defects in spearmint overexpression or RNAi lines
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: commercial spearmint variety; basil variety NA; N. sylvestris line NA
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative RNA-Seq of spearmint tissues (leaf, leaf stripped of PGTs, PGTs); small RNA-seq for miR858 in PGTs and leaf stripped of PGTs; accession NA
